description: AccrediTex project-local ReasoningBank — stores past decisions for pattern reuse

decisions.task: Implement role-based authentication with Firebase Auth
decisions.decision: Firebase Auth + custom claims for roles (admin, user, viewer). Store role in Firestore users/{uid} doc. useFirebaseAuth hook syncs auth state to useUserStore. Guard routes via AppRouter role check. Firestore rules enforce role access.
decisions.outcome: Role-based auth deployed. Admin, Technician, Viewer roles working. Protected routes stable.

decisions.task: Debug LPO system — verify Firestore queries and data consistency
decisions.decision: Audited all LPO-related files. Firestore queries in services layer were correct but missing composite index on (orgId, status, createdAt). Added index to firestore.indexes.json and redeployed.
decisions.outcome: LPO list queries now under 200ms. Data consistency verified.

decisions.task: Admin whitelabeling — global logo and system name customization
decisions.decision: Store branding in Firestore appSettings/{orgId}. CSS variable --user-primary drives primary color. App.tsx already reads appSettings.primaryColor on load. BrandingPanel component in admin settings writes to Firestore. useAppStore fetches on init.
decisions.outcome: Logo and system name configurable per organization. CSS variable updates in real-time without reload.

decisions.task: Hybrid access control — static roles + permission matrix
decisions.decision: Roles (Admin/Manager/User/Viewer) are static enums. Permissions are a matrix in Firestore orgPermissions/{orgId} with feature toggles. Role check is fast (in-memory), permission check queries Firestore. Combined with usePermissions hook. Firestore rules enforce both.
decisions.outcome: Granular permission matrix working. Admins can toggle feature access per role. Firestore rules block unauthorized access.

decisions.task: Fix deployment errors on Render — runtime dependency issues
decisions.decision: Render build failed: missing firebase-admin peer deps. Fix: add firebase-admin to dependencies (not devDependencies). Also: vite build --mode production not --mode develop in render deploy script. Added NODE_ENV=production to Render env vars.
decisions.outcome: Render deployment stable. Zero runtime errors post-fix.

decisions.task: Fix React hooks order error in PatientProfilePage — useRef in conditional
decisions.decision: useRef was called inside an if-block, violating React's rules of hooks. Fix: move useRef to top of component before any conditionals. Pattern: ALL hooks must be called unconditionally at the top level.
decisions.outcome: Hooks error resolved. Component renders correctly.

decisions.task: Add dark mode to existing components
decisions.decision: AccrediTex uses ThemeProvider (src/components/common/ThemeProvider.tsx). Dark classes use dark: prefix with brand tokens. Pattern: bg-brand-background dark:dark-brand-background. Toggle via ThemeProvider context. DO NOT use next-themes (wrong stack — this is Vite not Next.js).
decisions.outcome: Dark mode consistent across all components using brand tokens.

decisions.decision: Lighthouse 2026-02-28: LCP 2.1s (needs < 2.5s), FCP 1.4s, CLS 0.02. Top issues: 1) Heavy chart bundles not code-split (Recharts 180kb). 2) Firebase SDK loaded fully on unauthenticated routes. Fix: dynamic import Recharts, lazy load Firebase analytics only when authed.
decisions.outcome: Bundle reduced 23%. LCP improved to 1.8s. Lighthouse score 78→86.

patterns.name: Firebase Service Layer Pattern
patterns.pattern: ALL Firestore queries in src/services/ — never in components. Service functions return typed objects. Zustand store calls service functions. Components read from store only.

patterns.name: AccrediTex Dark Mode Pattern
patterns.pattern: Use ThemeProvider context + TailwindCSS v4 dark: prefix + brand tokens. Never use next-themes (Vite project). Classes: bg-brand-background dark:dark-brand-background, text-brand-text-primary dark:dark-brand-text-primary

patterns.name: Firestore Index For List Queries
patterns.pattern: Any query filtering on 2+ fields needs a composite index in firestore.indexes.json. Pattern: (orgId, status, createdAt DESC). Always check Firestore console for missing index errors before debugging queries.
